Corporate MA Associate Mid levelView Job ↗
2026-03-23
Miami, Florida · Corporate · 2020–2022 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Greenberg Traurig has an excellent opportunity for an M&A Associate to join the Corporate Practice of our Miami office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of four years of significant experience in corporate transactions with an emphasis on public and private M&A, including private equity. Candidates must have a strong working knowledge of acquisition agreements and the other documentation used in complex M&A and private equity transactions and proficiency in drafting the same. This position requires a candidate with strong interpersonal skills, a high degree of maturity, and a proven willingness to accept significant responsibility and manage a challenging workload within a fast-paced environment. Strong academic credentials and writing skills are essential. Show job description
Nelson Mullins law firm is looking for a corporate associate to join its Affordable Housing Team. As an AmLaw100 firm, this will offer a unique opportunity to a lateral associate who wants to be part of an expanding team. This opportunity is open to multiple large markets. As a Nelson Mullins associate, you’ll be joining the ranks of over 1,000 attorneys and policy advisors. Want to know more about Nelson Mullins? Check out our Firm Culture here. All candidates are asked to submit a resume, deal summary, and a law school transcript. You have at least 1 years of meaningful experience in affordable housing. You possess excellent academic credentials and strong writing, analytical, and communications skills and are a member of the bar in a state in which Nelson Mullins has an office. With over 1,000 attorneys and professionals, Nelson Mullins has strong roots in the business community and an appreciation for new directions in the business world. Job Description: Cooley is a leading global law firm with offices across the US, Europe and Asia. Our clients, ranging from entrepreneurs to emerging companies to Fortune 50 corporations, partner with us on transformative deals, complex IP and regulatory matters and high-stakes litigation where innovation meets the law. As a firm we constantly strive to deliver the highest quality work and superior service on every matter entrusted to us. Our lawyers are organized into two departments—Business and Litigation—which are further organized into practice groups. Given our uncompromising commitment to the quality of work that we produce, remaining on, if not defining, the leading edge of the law in a business and legal environment that is evolving daily, and working efficiently and cost-effectively, we are intently focused on enabling our practitioners and practice groups to the greatest extent possible. Tech Transactions Lateral Associate (Bay Area)View Job ↗
2026-03-20
San Francisco, California; Silicon Valley, California · Corporate · 2020–2022 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Baker Botts L.L.P. is seeking a mid-level associate with 4–6 years of experience to join our Technology Transactions practice in the Palo Alto or San Francisco office. The ideal candidate will have significant experience advising clients on a broad range of technology-focused commercial transactions, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating agreements involving the development, licensing, commercialization, and acquisition of technology and intellectual property. This role is well-suited for candidates seeking a mix of standalone commercial work and deal-driven technology transactions in fast-paced, highly strategic environments. Responsibilities: • Drafting and negotiating complex technology and intellectual property agreements, including SaaS, cloud services, licensing, and development agreements • Advising on the technology and IP aspects of M&A, private equity, and venture capital transactions, including due diligence, representations and warranties, IP structuring, and
Haley Flora in FP 195 NEW Also: Gunderson Dettmer Stough Villeneuve Franklin & Hachigian

Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher 1 role · 2 matches · best 186
Labor & Employment (OC)View Job ↗
2026-03-20
Orange County, California · Labor & Employment · 2017–2021 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
We are seeking an associate in law school class years 2017 through 2021 to join the labor and employment team. The practice is primarily employer-side litigation, with some advice and investigations work as well. Litigation is mostly class and representative actions, both wage and hour and discrimination, and experience in the class actions space would be helpful. Three years+ in a peer law firm in the employment space would likely have requisite experience. The annual compensation range for this position is between $310,000 and $435,000. The salary offered within this range will depend upon qualifications and other operational considerations. Benefits offered for this position include health care; retirement benefits; paid days off, including sick time, and vacation time; parental leave; basic life insurance; Flexible Spending Accounts; as well as discretionary, performance-based bonuses.

We are looking for an associate to join our Strategic Transactions and Licensing group (the “Tech Group”) in Austin, Los Angeles, Silicon Valley, San Francisco, or Ann Arbor*. Our practice offers early responsibility, direct client contact, and exposure to a wide variety of technology, life sciences, AI, financial services, and consumer products companies at all stages of growth. This role is well-suited for associates who want to accelerate their development as technology and commercial counselors while working with a collaborative, high-performing team. As a Tech Group associate, you will: * Draft and negotiate licensing, partnering, and commercial agreements * Provide intellectual property, privacy, and product counseling directly to clients * Manage transactions across all stages of our clients’ businesses, from formation and financings to buy- and sell-side acquisitions and beyond.. New York City, New York · Energy · ≤2022 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Hunton seeks an attorney with at least four years of project finance experience to join the Energy and Infrastructure team in a full-time partnership-track associate position in our New York office. Prior experience at a national or international law firm strongly preferred. Candidates must possess excellent oral and written communication skills, demonstrate initiative and potential for leadership, and have the judgment and confidence to work independently with clients and colleagues. New York bar membership is preferred, and in any event required within one year of joining the firm. EEO/drug-free workplace/E-Verify participant/Veteran/Disability. At the time of posting, the expected salary range for this position for full-time on-track associates is $310,000 to $435,000. Actual salary offers may vary based on a number of factors, including but not limited to, education, training, skill sets, licensure and certifications, practice area, experience, internal equity, and market data. The Executive Compensation and Employee Benefits Group of Reed Smith is currently seeking a junior to mid-level associate to join the firm’s New York, Pittsburgh, Boston or Atlanta offices. This role will work in tandem with a collaborative and experienced team whose practice covers nearly all types of employee benefit plans and programs, health and welfare benefit plans, and executive compensation arrangements. Reed Smith offers a challenging work environment and a total compensation package that includes a competitive salary, flexible benefits program, tuition assistance, and generous 401(k) plan. Reed Smith is an Equal Opportunity Employer with Core Values of Integrity, Excellence, Teamwork & Respect, Innovation, and Impact. Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for a mid-level Associate (4 – 6 years) in the Corporate Practice, Mergers and Acquisitions group, of our West Palm Beach office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Corporate Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. This is a full-time position based in our West Palm Beach office. This is an opportunity to work on sophisticated transactions for high-profile clients—both domestic and international—while enjoying the unique quality of life that South Florida offers. Spencer Fane 1 role · 1 match · best 164
Financial Services Litigation and Insolvency AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-19
Dallas, Texas · Bankruptcy · 2021–2024 · 1 elite match
Show job description
JOB TITLE: Financial Services Litigation and Insolvency Associate LOCATION: Plano, Texas office (hybrid work environment) POSITION SUMMARY: Spencer Fane currently has an opening in our PLANO, TEXAS OFFICE for a FINANCIAL SERVICES LITIGATION AND INSOLVENCY ASSOCIATE. The ideal candidate will have 2-5 years of litigation experience and have a strong interest in the insolvency practice, including state and federal litigation, commercial bankruptcy, receivership, and insolvency related litigation. The position will require regular court room appearances (both virtual and live) and the candidate must have the ability to handle appearances independently, interact skillfully with court personnel and opposing parties. The ideal candidate will have experience with all phases of commercial litigation, including fact investigation, legal research, drafting discovery, preparing pleadings, depositions, mediations, communicating with opposing counsel and clients, and appearing in court for both

ABOUT THE ROLE Clark Hill is seeking an attorney with at least three (3) years of Labor & Employment litigation experience to join its Labor & Employment team in the Los Angeles, CA office. This position focuses on single-plaintiff employment litigation and class action/PAGA cases. This is a full-time position with a hybrid work schedule. REQUIRED EXPERIENCE AND SKILL SET * Drafting pleadings, workplace policies, and discovery * Drafting and arguing motions, and conducting administrative hearings * Taking and defending depositions * Conducting employee trainings * Providing day-to-day advice and counseling to employers QUALIFICATIONS * Juris Doctor (JD) from an accredited law school * Must be currently licensed to practice law in the State of California or eligible for admission without examination. The Firm is ranked in the First Tier nationally in the category of Labor and Employment Litigation, as well as in both Employment Law and Labor Law on behalf of Management, in the U.S. News - Best Lawyers® “Best Law Firms”. This position is located in our Albany, NY location. For New York, the expected salary range for this position is between $145,000 - $205,000. This position is also eligible for annual bonuses. This position offers the opportunity for significant responsibility and client contact. Successful candidates should possess excellent drafting and analytical skills, great attention to detail, high level of organization, strong verbal and interpersonal skills, and the ability to manage multiple projects and people, at times across offices. Superior academic credentials and admission to the New York Bar are required. Large or mid-size law firm experience preferred. We currently expect 3 days in-office, including Wednesdays. Show job description
Kelley Drye & Warren LLP seeks an associate with 2-4 years of law firm experience to join its Employee Benefits and Executive Compensation (EBEC) practice. The EBEC group is tight knit and values a collaborative work environment. The group currently consists of three partners, and the practice is broad-based with corporate and non-profit clients in an array of industries. A successful associate will have executive compensation (409A), qualified plan (ERISA), and transactional experience. Responsibilities will include: * Assisting clients in developing, implementing, administering, and terminating qualified and non-qualified retirement plans, health and welfare benefit plans, and other compensatory arrangements, including stock option plans, restricted stock, equity-based awards, omnibus equity plans, cash bonus plans, employee stock purchase plans, executive retirement plans, and other deferred compensation arrangements. * Handling the benefits aspects of mergers, acquisitions,

Show job description
Ropers Majeski’s growing and dynamic litigation practice has an opportunity for a California-licensed Litigation Attorney with a minimum of three years of commercial and general liability litigation experience. The position offers flexibility to work remotely from anywhere in the US or in person in one of our US-based offices. Founded 75 years ago, Ropers Majeski maintains offices internationally and throughout the United States. Skills and Educational Requirements JD with excellent academic credentials Minimum of three years of experience working on general liability matters Ability to handle complex litigation matters from inception to trial, including motion writing, preparation of pleadings and briefs, managing discovery and written perpetuate depositions, analyzing professional liability coverage, and conducting legal research and analysis The ideal candidate will have experience in both state and federal court proceedings Proficient in Microsoft Word, Outlook, Excel, and e-discov

Atlanta, Georgia · Corporate · ≤2024 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Are you looking to join a tenured team at an AmLaw100 firm? Nelson Mullins is seeking an associate to join its corporate group in its Atlanta office. Firm attorneys in Atlanta have worked on many business and legal developments in the Southeast and beyond, including national class action court cases, formation of public companies, financial institution capital transactions, mergers and acquisitions, and work on important pro bono cases. The Firm moved to its current location at 201 17th Street NW in 2007 as the first tenant in a new building located at Atlantic Station, a brownfield reclamation and mixed-used development that houses everyday amenities including alternative transportation options within walking distance. The office meets the U.S. Green Building Council’s Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) certification. Carlton Fields is seeking an associate with 4-5 years of business litigation experience within a law firm and must be admitted to practice in Washington, D.C., Maryland, and Virginia. Excellent academic credentials and outstanding writing skills are required. Law Review or Clerkship experience is a plus. Experience with federal court litigation is also a plus. The ideal candidate will have strong interpersonal and communication skills, and the ability to work effectively as part of a team, while being able to work individually, in an environment where drive and client service are top priorities. To apply, please submit your cover letter, resume, transcript, and a writing sample. Carlton Fields is an Equal Opportunity Employer/Disabled/Veterans. Salary Range: $195,000 - $200,000

Show job description
Nelson Mullins law firm is seeking to add LITIGATION ATTORNEYS to its Litigation group. This is a hybrid or in-office position, and we are open to considering candidates in San Diego, Los Angeles, or Torrance. As an AmLaw100 firm, this is a unique opportunity to join an expanding practice in a growing office. San Diego attorneys provide counsel to manufacturers across the nation facing single-plaintiff claims or mass action litigation. They serve as national coordinating counsel in state and federal courts in matters involving medical devices, pharmaceuticals, automobiles, industrial machinery, and construction equipment, among other products. The California-based attorneys also represent auto manufacturers in consumer fraud cases. All candidates are asked to submit a cover letter, resume, and a copy of their law school transcript. 2026 Post-Clerkship Litigation AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-12
New York City, New York · Litigation · ≤2025 · 7 elite matches
Show job description
Patterson Belknap Webb & Tyler LLP is accepting resumes from candidates presently serving judicial clerkships. All applicants should be interested in joining our Litigation Department with start dates beginning in Fall 2026. The expected salary range for this position is $225,000 - $365,000 per year, plus competitive benefits and bonus eligibility. Associate base compensation at Patterson Belknap Webb & Tyler is typically lock-step, based on experience. All applicants must submit a resume, law school transcript, and a writing sample. Your writing sample should: Be no more than 1250 words. An excerpt from a longer work is fine, but please choose an excerpt that a reader can understand without the context of the larger piece. Be your own original work, with no editing from others. If you do not have a writing sample that meets this requirement, please choose a sample with as little external editing as possible, and include a note with your sample informing us (i) who edited the sample an

Show job description
DLA Piper is, at its core, bold, exceptional, collaborative and supportive. Our people are the backbone, heart and soul of our firm. Wherever you are in your professional journey, DLA Piper is a place you can engage in meaningful work and grow your career. Let’s see what we can achieve. Together. Summary DLA Piper is seeking a mid-level associate to join its corporate group. This associate will be focusing primarily on working with emerging growth companies and the institutional investors that support them. The ideal candidate will have at least three years’ experience representing both institutional investors and issuers in venture capital financings, as well as experience with M&A transactions and advising emerging growth companies on a variety of day-to-day corporate matters. This position is open to our Los Angeles, Palo Alto, San Francisco, New York, Washington DC, and Austin offices. We are looking to add a team member with broad experience working on M&A, private equity transactions, private investments, complex restructurings, joint ventures and other transactions. We strive to develop exceptional attorneys across all levels by providing them with the tools and experiences needed to build successful careers. ABOUT THE PRACTICE GROUP Latham’s Capital Markets Practice helps clients raise billions of dollars each year through all forms of securities products. 2026-03-11
District of Columbia · Litigation, Antitrust · ≤2022 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Venable LLP is seeking a midlevel to senior litigation associate with a minimum of four years of experience in complex litigation, ideally in some or all antitrust cases or similar complex litigation, to join a litigation team in the Washington, DC office. In this role the associate will be an integral part of a team dedicated to supporting a portfolio of antitrust litigation matters. This model offers an exceptional level of training on all aspects of antitrust litigation and opportunities to grow in subject matter expertise, litigation skills, and trial experience. The qualified candidate has substantial experience in all aspects of complex litigation, including managing discovery and e-discovery, depositions, expert witness preparation, and motions and working in multi-party litigation, ideally class actions. Experience before enforcement agencies such as the DOJ, FTC, and state attorneys general is a plus. Preference for experience in a law firm setting. All candidates should po

Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for a Junior to Mid-Level Associate in the Litigation Practice of our Los Angeles office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently ranked among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Litigation Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from a variety of industries across the country and sometimes internationally. Job Requirements: Candidates should have between two and five years of litigation experience. Candidates must have excellent academic credentials and strong research, writing and oral advocacy skills. Who We Are MoFo is a destination law firm for talented, ambitious attorneys who value collaboration, teamwork, and commitment to excellence. We are known around the world not only for delivering innovative yet practical client service but also for serving as an incubator for our top-notch talent. MoFo consistently receives top-tier rankings from leading directories and publications, speaking to our legal capabilities, dedication to creating an inclusive workplace, and commitment to our communities. Our Finance Practice Group As a co-chair of the Finance practice group and hiring partner for this position, Dario Avram states "associates in the Finance group have the opportunity to work on complex and fast paced financing transactions, together with a diverse group of smart and collaborative colleagues." The ideal candidate will have practiced at least two (2) years in the area of corporate and business law with experience handling a variety of corporate and financial transactions and issues, including corporate acquisitions, sales, mergers, corporate restructurings, contracts, and corporate governance matters. Private equity experience is a plus. Admission to Massachusetts, Connecticut, or Rhode Island bar is required (or must be obtained within 6-12 months). The position offers an excellent opportunity to assume significant responsibility and hands-on experience in a collaborative and team-oriented work environment. District of Columbia · Antitrust · 2020–2023 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
The Washington, DC, office of Hogan Lovells’ global antitrust practice is seeking a lateral candidate with at least 3 years of experience, which could include law firm experience, government experience, or clerkship experience.  Our practice handles a wide range of domestic and cross-border antitrust and competition matters, including litigation, government investigations, merger clearance, and counseling.  We are seeking a candidate that would focus primarily on the antitrust litigation side of our practice.  Our caseload includes major consumer class actions (such as In Re Blue Cross Blue Shield Antitrust Litigation and In re Mylan EpiPen Litigation), non-class action antitrust lawsuits, litigation with state and federal enforcers (such as US v. Agristats), and international cartel investigations.  Litigation experience (either plaintiff or defense side) is required.  Antitrust experience, trial experience, and/or an economics background is preferred. A resume or CV is a must for our

Job Description: The Tysons office of McGuireWoods seeks an Associate to join its Financial Services and Securities Enforcement Department. The cornerstone of McGuireWoods’ financial services and securities enforcement group is the team’s ability to effectively represent the interests of the vital, growing and increasingly complex financial services sector. This representation includes securities, lender liability, regulatory issues, loan workouts, foreclosures, class actions and credit card-related matters. Lawyers’ experience encompasses the expanding services offered by financial institutions, including recent cases involving securities fraud, failed asset securitizations and defaulted syndicated loans. The firm also represents banks in connection with commercial disputes, business torts and intellectual property matters. Description We are seeking a highly motivated litigation associate to join our Intellectual Property practice group in New York or Chicago. Ideal candidates should have 4-7 years of experience at a law firm, with a strong track record handling all phases of litigation, from pre-litigation counseling and claim assessment through discovery and trial. Responsibilities will include all aspects of litigation, including drafting pleadings, managing discovery, including large e-discovery projects, taking and defending depositions, motion practice, and trial preparation. Candidates will be expected to focus on intellectual property litigation relating to trademark, copyright, trade secrets and patent disputes, but we will consider candidates with a strong federal court commercial litigation background, who are interested in focusing on technology and intellectual property disputes. Show job description
Lewis Brisbois, a full-service Am Law 100 firm, is actively seeking a litigation associate attorney to join its Washington, DC office. This role offers the opportunity to work on a variety of cases, including general liability, professional liability, and labor & employment. The ideal candidate will have one (1) to seven (7) years of civil litigation experience and should have experience managing discovery, as well as outstanding research, brief writing, and oral advocacy skills. We are looking for candidates who want significant responsibility to handle court appearances, depositions, motion practice, and case files independently. Admission to the DC and Maryland Bars is required (admission to the Virginia bar is a plus). We offer competitive compensation and in addition, Lewis Brisbois offers a range of benefits including medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and 401k with employer match. Experience: 4 to 6 years in business litigation law. Qualifications Experience in litigating complex business disputes and ability to manage matters with increasing independence while delivering a high-quality work product in a fast-paced environment. Prior experience leading court conferences in state and/or federal court. Prior experience conducting depositions. Prior experience managing discovery. Responsibilities Handle all phases of business and commercial litigation matters. Draft pleadings, dispositive and non-dispositive motions, briefs, and discovery materials. Appear in court for hearings, conferences, and motion arguments. Assist with trial preparation, arbitration proceedings, and settlement negotiations. Are you looking to join a tenured team at an AmLaw100 firm? Nelson Mullins is seeking an associate to join its corporate group. As a Nelson Mullins associate, you’ll be joining the ranks of over 1,000 attorneys and policy advisors, a Firm culture that will offer you a broad practice platform that is also personal enough to provide individual client attention no matter your office location. Want to know more about Nelson Mullins? Check out our Firm Culture here. All candidates are asked to submit a cover letter, resume, and a copy of their law school transcript. You have at least three years of meaningful experience working at a midsize or large law firm on public company and private company securities transactions and 1934 Act reporting requirements, with additional experience in mergers & acquisitions a plus. New York Office - Private Equity/M&A Mid-Level AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-05
New York City, New York · Corporate · 2020–2023 · 5 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn’s New York office is looking for a corporate associate to join our private equity and mergers and acquisitions group. Candidates should have at least three years of M&A and/or private equity transactional experience in the corporate department of a top-tier law firm. Ideal candidates will have experience representing financial sponsors and strategic buyers/sellers in connection with leveraged buyouts, sell-side transactions, growth equity and minority investments, and related strategic transactions. All candidates must be admitted to the New York Bar. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

Polsinelli is seeking a highly qualified and motivated mid-level associate for our Trademark/Copyright/Branding Practice Group in Chicago, Kansas City, St. Louis, New York or Washington, DC office. Ideal candidates will possess at least two to five years of legal experience in U.S. trademark clearance, prosecution, portfolio management, and TTAB work, copyright counseling, and trademark-specific litigation experience. Some international prosecution experience and licensing and acquisition agreement experience is preferred. Polsinelli is particularly interested in candidates who have the ambition, drive, and team orientation to help grow our national practice. Show job description
Norton Rose Fulbright is seeking a Lawyer to join its Projects Development team. The ideal candidate will have 4 to 10+ years of relevant experience and a strong background in construction, procurement, and project development, with location flexibility across all of our US offices. The ideal candidate will have a background in project development within the power, energy, oil & gas, or chemical sectors, or extensive construction experience, particularly with large-scale infrastructure projects. Experience should include negotiating engineering, procurement, and construction agreements, equipment procurement agreements (such as PV or BESS or turbine procurement), operations and maintenance agreements, long term services agreements, and other development agreements. This role provides an opportunity to work on high-profile projects in renewable energy, energy storage, power generation, and other energy transition technologies and will sit on our market-leading Projects team. Show job description
Associate – Litigation - D.C. Build your big career with the firm that does Big Law, Better. McDermott Will & Schulte is a leading global law firm that brings together more than 1,750 lawyers and 1,450 business professionals. We celebrate excellence, collaboration, and community and have been recognized as a top workplace by USA Today, Fortune, The American Lawyer, Vault and others. We are also certified by Great Place to Work. Here, we don’t just invest in your future, we accelerate your career – wherever it may lead. That includes supporting you both in and outside of the office. With us, you’ll find: A firm where everyone belongs: Our award-winning culture prioritizes warmth and authenticity — we encourage you to be yourself! Enthusiasm for all perspectives: We’re smarter and stronger when everyone has a voice and a seat at the table. Arnold & Porter Kaye Scholer 1 role · 4 matches · best 194
Government Contracts & National Security Associate - Mid-LevelView Job ↗
2026-03-04
District of Columbia · Government · 2020–2023 · 4 elite matches
Show job description
Arnold & Porter’s Government Contracts & National Security Practice Group is seeking a mid-level associate for the Washington, D.C. office. Arnold & Porter’s Chambers/Legal 500 Band 1 (market leading) Government Contracts & National Security team has a broad mix of litigation, investigation, export control, cybersecurity, mergers and acquisition, and counseling work. Some examples of the group’s recent work include: litigating multi-billion dollar, complex contract award challenges for large aerospace and defense companies and handling the negotiation of novel agreements with the government (including OTAs, CRADAs, and TIAs). We also frequently advise both recipients and subrecipients of grants (including the Inflation Reduction Act and CHIPS Act) in the interpretation of grant requirements and compliance, as well as federal audits, enforcement actions, and other controversies with awarding agencies. The work is exciting, often quite relevant to ongoing world issues, and fast paced. Qu

Job Description: The Tysons office of McGuireWoods is seeking a mid-level Litigation Associate to support both Virginia-based and national civil litigation. The Tyson’s litigation team delivers comprehensive, high-level counsel to universities, Fortune 100 companies, and prominent individuals facing complex legal and reputational challenges. With nationally-recognized strength in litigation, the firm guides clients through high-stakes enforcement matters — including congressional investigations — and related civil litigation by combining deep legal experience, bipartisan relationships, and strategic crisis management. McGuireWoods, one of the world’s leading law firms, has provided legal solutions to corporate, individual and nonprofit clients since 1834. Along with excellent benefits, McGuireWoods offers our attorneys a competitive salary with hours and merit bonus opportunities and billable hours credit for pro bono work. Healthcare Litigation AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-04
District of Columbia; Atlanta, Georgia · Litigation · 2022–2024 · 1 elite match
Show job description
AGG, an Am Law 200 firm with more than 200 attorneys with offices in Atlanta and Washington, D.C., is seeking an associate with 2-4 years of experience in healthcare litigation to join its nationally recognized Healthcare practice. The ideal candidate has 2-4 years of experience in a law firm setting, with specific experience representing healthcare providers in matters involving the False Claims Act, Certificate of Need proceedings and regulatory disputes. Successful candidates will have excellent academic credentials and legal research, writing, and analytical skills as well as strong communication and interpersonal skills. This position can be located in our Atlanta or Washington, D.C. The expected salary range for this position is $200,000 to $275,000. Actual compensation offers may vary based on a number of factors, including but not limited to experience, training, and skill sets.

Job Description At K&L Gates, we are looking for smart, imaginative and hard-working people with diverse backgrounds, experiences and ideas to join us. Perhaps our search for talented visionaries and your search for important and impactful work lead to the same place. We are seeking a midlevel Asset Management and Investment Funds associate to join the Boston, New York or Washington D.C. office, with a preference for candidates who will be located in the Boston office. The ideal candidate will have two to six years of experience with open-end (mutual) funds, closed-end funds and/or exchange traded funds (ETFs) and experience representing investment companies, investment advisers, and board members on matters arising under U.S. federal securities law, particularly the Investment Company Act of 1940, the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, the Securities Act of 1933, the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and their related rules and regulations. Show job description
The Woodland Hills office of Fisher Phillips seeks associates with at least five years of experience to join our wage and hour litigation team. Our California team regularly defends employers in complex, high-exposure wage and hour class, collective, and representative actions in state and federal courts throughout the state. Ideal candidates will have substantial experience litigating claims under the California Labor Code, the Private Attorneys General Act (PAGA), and the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Candidates must demonstrate meaningful experience handling wage and hour class and representative matters from inception through resolution, including: Drafting and responding to complaints, answers, and dispositive motions Managing written discovery Taking and defending depositions Preparing and arguing motions, including class certification and decertification motions Litigating and strategizing PAGA representative actions Analyzing exemption classifications, regular rate calculati

Attorney-Environmental AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-02
Albany, New York; New York City, New York · Environmental · ≤2023 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Goldberg Segalla LLP, a national law firm with more than 23 offices in 11 states, has an immediate opportunity for an associate to join our Environmental practice group in any of our New York offices. THE QUALIFICATIONS Qualified candidates will be admitted in New York and have 3+ years of experience taking and defending depositions, drafting and arguing dispositive motions, and preparing for mediations and trial. The ideal candidate writes effectively, thinks strategically, and has prior experience in Enviornmental law or general laibility. THE BENEFITS The annualized salary range for this position is $85,000-$165,000. Actual pay may be adjusted based on experience and other job related factors permitted by law. This position also offers bonuses and a full benefits package. Our benefits package includes, but is not limited to, health insurance, dental insurance, life insurance, a 401(k) plan, flexible spending, and more. THE FIRM The Goldberg Segalla approach to teamwork and mentoring

Wilson Elser is a leading defense litigation law firm with more than 1400 attorneys in 43 offices throughout the United States. Founded in 1978, we rank among the top 100 law firms identified by The American Lawyer and 36 in the National Law Journal’s survey of the nation’s largest law firms. Our firm is committed to attracting and retaining professionals who value each other and the service we provide by embracing Teamwork, Collaboration, Client Service, and Innovation. If you are a motivated professional looking for a long-term fit where you can grow in a role, and will be valued and empowered, then we invite you to apply to our National Litigation & Claims Management Attorney position in our Chicago Office. This position offers a flexible, hybrid working arrangement. Capital Markets AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-02
Silicon Valley, California · Corporate · 2020–2023 · 1 elite match
Show job description
O’Melveny is seeking a mid-level associate Capital Markets Associate (3rd – 6th year). Ideal candidates will have a range of experience typical for our Capital Markets practice, including securities offerings and assisting public companies with reporting obligations. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing and diverse team. Our Capital Markets Practice Group has substantial experience running the most complicated and high-profile deals. We view challenges from all perspectives—companies, investment banks, and regulators—to navigate deals seamlessly from launch through pricing and closing. So tell us, what do you want to achieve? The salary range in San Francisco for this role is $260,000 - $390,000 and represents the firm’s good faith minimum and maximum range for this role at the time of posting. San Francisco, California · Litigation · 2020–2022 · 1 elite match
Show job description
The San Francisco, California office of FBT Gibbons is seeking a Business Litigation Associate. FBT Gibbons LLP is a national, full-service law firm formed through the combination of Frost Brown Todd and Gibbons, bringing together more than 800 attorneys across 25 offices. Our attorneys offer comprehensive legal solutions to clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies and global multinationals to startups and nonprofits. FBT Gibbons distinguishes itself as a dynamic, forward-moving firm with a coast-to-coast footprint and a reputation for excellence across multiple practice areas and industries. Our reputation is further reinforced by its numerous Chambers and Best Lawyers ranked practices and attorneys. We are a people‑first firm recognized for our award-winning inclusive culture and a strong commitment to community and pro bono work.
